translations.text: Commit your way to Yahweh. Trust also in him, and he will do this:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: he will make your righteousness go out as the light, and your justice as the noon day sun.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Rest in Yahweh, and wait patiently for him. Don’t fret because of him who prospers in his way, because of the man who makes wicked plots happen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Cease from anger, and forsake wrath. Don’t fret, it leads only to evildoing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For evildoers shall be cut off, but those who wait for Yahweh shall inherit the land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For yet a little while, and the wicked will be no more. Yes, though you look for his place, he isn’t there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the humble shall inherit the land, and shall delight themselves in the abundance of peace.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wicked plots against the just, and gnashes at him with his teeth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Lord will laugh at him, for he sees that his day is coming.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wicked have drawn out the sword, and have bent their bow, to cast down the poor and needy, to kill those who are upright on the path.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their sword shall enter into their own heart. Their bows shall be broken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Better is a little that the righteous has, than the abundance of many wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the arms of the wicked shall be broken, but Yahweh upholds the righteous.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh knows the days of the perfect. Their inheritance shall be forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall not be disappointed in the time of evil. In the days of famine they shall be satisfied.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the wicked shall perish. The enemies of Yahweh shall be like the beauty of the fields. They will vanish — vanish like smoke.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wicked borrow, and don’t pay back, but the righteous give generously.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For such as are blessed by him shall inherit the land. Those who are cursed by him shall be cut off.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man’s goings are established by Yahweh. He delights in his way.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Though he stumble, he shall not fall, for Yahweh holds him up with his hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have been young, and now am old, yet I have not seen the righteous forsaken, nor his children begging for bread.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All day long he deals graciously, and lends. His offspring  is blessed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Depart from evil, and do good. Live securely forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Yahweh loves justice, and doesn’t forsake his saints. They are preserved forever, but the children of the wicked shall be cut off.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The righteous shall inherit the land, and live in it forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The mouth of the righteous talks of wisdom. His tongue speaks justice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The law of his God is in his heart. None of his steps shall slide.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wicked watches the righteous, and seeks to kill him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wait for Yahweh, and keep his way, and he will exalt you to inherit the land. When the wicked are cut off, you shall see it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have seen the wicked in great power, spreading himself like a green tree in its native soil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he passed away, and behold, he was not. Yes, I sought him, but he could not be found.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Mark the perfect man, and see the upright, for there is a future for the man of peace.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As for transgressors, they shall be destroyed together. The future of the wicked shall be cut off.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the salvation of the righteous is from Yahweh. He is their stronghold in the time of trouble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh helps them, and rescues them. He rescues them from the wicked, and saves them, Because they have taken refuge in him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A Psalm by David, for a memorial. Yahweh, don’t rebuke me in your wrath, neither chasten me in your hot displeasure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For your arrows have pierced me, your hand presses hard on me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is no soundness in my flesh because of your indignation, neither is there any health in my bones because of my sin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For my iniquities have gone over my head. As a heavy burden, they are too heavy for me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My wounds are loathsome and corrupt, because of my foolishness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am pained and bowed down greatly. I go mourning all day long.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For my waist is filled with burning. There is no soundness in my flesh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am faint and severely bruised. I have groaned by reason of the anguish of my heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Lord, all my desire is before you. My groaning is not hidden from you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My heart throbs. My strength fails me. As for the light of my eyes, it has also left me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My lovers and my friends stand aloof from my plague. My kinsmen stand far away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They also who seek after my life lay snares. Those who seek my hurt speak mischievous things, and meditate deceits all day long.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I, as a deaf man, don’t hear. I am as a mute man who doesn’t open his mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, I am as a man who doesn’t hear, in whose mouth are no reproofs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For in you, Yahweh, do I hope. You will answer, Lord my God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I said, “Don’t let them gloat over me, or exalt themselves over me when my foot slips.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I am ready to fall. My pain is continually before me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I will declare my iniquity. I will be sorry for my sin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But my enemies are vigorous and many. Those who hate me without reason are numerous.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They who also render evil for good are adversaries to me, because I follow what is good.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t forsake me, Yahweh. My God, don’t be far from me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hurry to help me, Lord, my salvation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. For Jeduthun. A Psalm by David. I said, “I will watch my ways, so that I don’t sin with my tongue. I will keep my mouth with a bridle while the wicked is before me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I was mute with silence. I held my peace, even from good. My sorrow was stirred.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My heart was hot within me. While I meditated, the fire burned. I spoke with my tongue:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Yahweh, show me my end, what is the measure of my days. Let me know how frail I am.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, you have made my days hand widths. My lifetime is as nothing before you. Surely every man stands as a breath.” Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Surely every man walks like a shadow. Surely they busy themselves in vain. He heaps up, and doesn’t know who shall gather.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now, Lord, what do I wait for? My hope is in you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Deliver me from all my transgressions. Don’t make me the reproach of the foolish.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I was mute. I didn’t open my mouth, because you did it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Remove your scourge away from me. I am overcome by the blow of your hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When you rebuke and correct man for iniquity, You consume his wealth like a moth. Surely every man is but a breath.” Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Hear my prayer, Yahweh, and give ear to my cry. Don’t be silent at my tears. For I am a stranger with you, a foreigner, as all my fathers were.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Oh spare me, that I may recover strength, before I go away, and exist no more.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. I waited patiently for Yahweh. He turned to me, and heard my cry.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He brought me up also out of a horrible pit, out of the miry clay. He set my feet on a rock, and gave me a firm place to stand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has put a new song in my mouth, even praise to our God. Many shall see it, and fear, and shall trust in Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the man who makes Yahweh his trust, and doesn’t respect the proud, nor such as turn aside to lies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Many, Yahweh, my God, are the wonderful works which you have done, and your thoughts which are toward us. They can’t be declared back to you. If I would declare and speak of them, they are more than can be counted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sacrifice and offering you didn’t desire. You have opened my ears. You have not required burnt offering and sin offering.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then I said, “Behold, I have come. It is written about me in the book in the scroll.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I delight to do your will, my God. Yes, your law is within my heart.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have proclaimed glad news of righteousness in the great assembly. Behold, I will not seal my lips, Yahweh, you know.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have not hidden your righteousness within my heart. I have declared your faithfulness and your salvation. I have not concealed your loving kindness and your truth from the great assembly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t withhold your tender mercies from me, Yahweh. Let your loving kindness and your truth continually preserve me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For innumerable evils have surrounded me. My iniquities have overtaken me, so that I am not able to look up. They are more than the hairs of my head. My heart has failed me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Be pleased, Yahweh, to deliver me. Hurry to help me, Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let them be disappointed and confounded together who seek after my soul to destroy it. Let them be turned backward and brought to dishonor who delight in my hurt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let them be desolate by reason of their shame that tell me, “Aha! Aha!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let all those who seek you rejoice and be glad in you. Let such as love your salvation say continually, “Let Yahweh be exalted!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I am poor and needy. May the Lord think about me. You are my help and my deliverer. Don’t delay, my God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. Blessed is he who considers the poor. Yahweh will deliver him in the day of evil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh will preserve him, and keep him alive. He shall be blessed on the earth, and he will not surrender him to the will of his enemies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh will sustain him on his sickbed, and restore him from his bed of illness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I said, “Yahweh, have mercy on me! Heal me, for I have sinned against you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My enemies speak evil against me: “When will he die, and his name perish?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If he comes to see me, he speaks falsehood. His heart gathers iniquity to itself. When he goes abroad, he tells it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All who hate me whisper together against me. They imagine the worst for me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “An evil disease”, they say, “has afflicted him. Now that he lies he shall rise up no more.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, my own familiar friend, in whom I trusted, who ate bread with me, has lifted up his heel against me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But you, Yahweh, have mercy on me, and raise me up, that I may repay them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By this I know that you delight in me, because my enemy doesn’t triumph over me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As for me, you uphold me in my integrity, and set me in your presence forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed be Yahweh, the God of Israel, from everlasting and to everlasting! Amen and amen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. A contemplation by the sons of Korah. As the deer pants for the water brooks, so my soul pants after you, God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My soul thirsts for God, for the living God. When shall I come and appear before God?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My tears have been my food day and night, while they continually ask me, “Where is your God?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These things I remember, and pour out my soul within me, how I used to go with the crowd, and led them to God’s house, with the voice of joy and praise, a multitude keeping a holy day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why are you in despair, my soul? Why are you disturbed within me? Hope in God! For I shall still praise him for the saving help of his presence.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My God, my soul is in despair within me. Therefore I remember you from the land of the Jordan, the heights of Hermon, from the hill Mizar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Deep calls to deep at the noise of your waterfalls. All your waves and your billows have swept over me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh  will command his loving kindness in the daytime. In the night his song shall be with me: a prayer to the God of my life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will ask God, my rock, “Why have you forgotten me? Why do I go mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As with a sword in my bones, my adversaries reproach me, while they continually ask me, “Where is your God?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why are you in despair, my soul? Why are you disturbed within me? Hope in God! For I shall still praise him, the saving help of my countenance, and my God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Vindicate me, God, and plead my cause against an ungodly nation. Oh, deliver me from deceitful and wicked men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you are the God of my strength. Why have you rejected me? Why do I go mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Oh, send out your light and your truth. Let them lead me. Let them bring me to your holy hill, To your tents.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then I will go to the altar of God, to God, my exceeding joy. I will praise you on the harp, God, my God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why are you in despair, my soul? Why are you disturbed within me? Hope in God! For I shall still praise him: my Savior, my helper, and my God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. By the sons of Korah. A contemplative psalm. We have heard with our ears, God; our fathers have told us, what work you did in their days, in the days of old.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You drove out the nations with your hand, but you planted them. You afflicted the peoples, but you spread them abroad.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they didn’t get the land in possession by their own sword, neither did their own arm save them; but your right hand, and your arm, and the light of your face, because you were favorable to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You are my King, God. Command victories for Jacob!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Through you, will we push down our adversaries. Through your name, will we tread them under who rise up against us.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I will not trust in my bow, neither shall my sword save me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But you have saved us from our adversaries, and have shamed those who hate us.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In God we have made our boast all day long, we will give thanks to your name forever. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But now you rejected us, and brought us to dishonor, and don’t go out with our armies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You make us turn back from the adversary. Those who hate us take plunder for themselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have made us like sheep for food, and have scattered us among the nations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You sell your people for nothing, and have gained nothing from their sale.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You make us a reproach to our neighbors, a scoffing and a derision to those who are around us.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You make us a byword among the nations, a shaking of the head among the peoples.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All day long my dishonor is before me, and shame covers my face,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At the taunt of one who reproaches and verbally abuses, because of the enemy and the avenger.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All this has come on us, yet have we not forgotten you, Neither have we been false to your covenant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Our heart has not turned back, neither have our steps strayed from your path,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Though you have crushed us in the haunt of jackals, and covered us with the shadow of death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If we have forgotten the name of our God, or spread out our hands to a strange god;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: won’t God search this out? For he knows the secrets of the heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, for your sake we are killed all day long. We are regarded as sheep for the slaughter.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wake up! Why do you sleep, Lord?  Arise! Don’t reject us forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why do you hide your face, and forget our affliction and our oppression?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For our soul is bowed down to the dust. Our body clings to the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Rise up to help us. Redeem us for your loving kindness’ sake.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. Set to “The Lilies.” A contemplation by the sons of Korah. A wedding song. My heart overflows with a noble theme. I recite my verses for the king. My tongue is like the pen of a skillful writer.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You are the most excellent of the sons of men. Grace has anointed your lips, therefore God has blessed you forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Strap your sword on your thigh, mighty one: your splendor and your majesty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In your majesty ride on victoriously on behalf of truth, humility, and righteousness. Let your right hand display awesome deeds.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your arrows are sharp. The nations fall under you, with arrows in the heart of the king’s enemies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your throne, God, is forever and ever. A scepter of equity is the scepter of your kingdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have loved righteousness, and hated wickedness. Therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your fellows.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All your garments smell like myrrh, aloes, and cassia. Out of ivory palaces stringed instruments have made you glad.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Kings’ daughters are among your honorable women. At your right hand the queen stands in gold of Ophir.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Listen, daughter, consider, and turn your ear. Forget your own people, and also your father’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the king will desire your beauty, honor him, for he is your lord.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The daughter of Tyre comes with a gift. The rich among the people entreat your favor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The princess inside is all glorious. Her clothing is interwoven with gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She shall be led to the king in embroidered work. The virgins, her companions who follow her, shall be brought to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With gladness and rejoicing they shall be led. They shall enter into the king’s palace.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your sons will take the place of your fathers. You shall make them princes in all the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will make your name to be remembered in all generations. Therefore the peoples shall give you thanks forever and ever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. By the sons of Korah. According to Alamoth. God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore we won’t be afraid, though the earth changes, though the mountains are shaken into the heart of the seas;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: though its waters roar and are troubled, though the mountains tremble with their swelling. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is a river, the streams of which make the city of God glad, the holy place of the tents of the Most High.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God is within her. She shall not be moved. God will help her at dawn.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The nations raged. The kingdoms were moved. He lifted his voice, and the earth melted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh of Armies is with us. The God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Come, see Yahweh’s works, what desolations he has made in the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He makes wars cease to the end of the earth. He breaks the bow, and shatters the spear. He burns the chariots in the fire.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Be still, and know that I am God. I will be exalted among the nations. I will be exalted in the earth.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh of Armies is with us. The God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by the sons of Korah. Oh clap your hands, all you nations. Shout to God with the voice of triumph!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Yahweh Most High is awesome. He is a great King over all the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He subdues nations under us, and peoples under our feet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He chooses our inheritance for us, the glory of Jacob whom he loved. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God has gone up with a shout, Yahweh with the sound of a trumpet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sing praises to God, sing praises. Sing praises to our King, sing praises.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For God is the King of all the earth. Sing praises with understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God reigns over the nations. God sits on his holy throne.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The princes of the peoples are gathered together, the people of the God of Abraham. For the shields of the earth belong to God. He is greatly exalted!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A Song. A Psalm by the sons of Korah. Great is Yahweh, and greatly to be praised, in the city of our God, in his holy mountain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Beautiful in elevation, the joy of the whole earth, is Mount Zion, on the north sides, the city of the great King.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God has shown himself in her citadels as a refuge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For, behold, the kings assembled themselves, they passed by together.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They saw it, then they were amazed. They were dismayed. They hurried away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Trembling took hold of them there, pain, as of a woman in travail.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With the east wind, you break the ships of Tarshish.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As we have heard, so we have seen, in the city of Yahweh of Armies, in the city of our God. God will establish it forever. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: We have thought about your loving kindness, God, in the middle of your temple.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As is your name, God, so is your praise to the ends of the earth. Your right hand is full of righteousness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let Mount Zion be glad! Let the daughters of Judah rejoice, Because of your judgments.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Walk about Zion, and go around her. Number its towers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Mark well her bulwarks. Consider her palaces, that you may tell it to the next generation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For this God is our God forever and ever. He will be our guide even to death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by the sons of Korah. Hear this, all you peoples. Listen, all you inhabitants of the world,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: both low and high, rich and poor together.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My mouth will speak words of wisdom. My heart shall utter understanding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will incline my ear to a proverb. I will open my riddle on the harp.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why should I fear in the days of evil, when iniquity at my heels surrounds me?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who trust in their wealth, and boast in the multitude of their riches —

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: none of them can by any means redeem his brother, nor give God a ransom for him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the redemption of their life is costly, no payment is ever enough,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that he should live on forever, that he should not see corruption.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he sees that wise men die; likewise the fool and the senseless perish, and leave their wealth to others.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their inward thought is that their houses will endure forever, and their dwelling places to all generations. They name their lands after themselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But man, despite his riches, doesn’t endure. He is like the animals that perish.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This is the destiny of those who are foolish, and of those who approve their sayings. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They are appointed as a flock for Sheol.  Death shall be their shepherd. The upright shall have dominion over them in the morning. Their beauty shall decay in Sheol,  far from their mansion.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But God will redeem my soul from the power of Sheol, for he will receive me. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t be afraid when a man is made rich, when the glory of his house is increased;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for when he dies he will carry nothing away. His glory won’t descend after him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Though while he lived he blessed his soul — and men praise you when you do well for yourself —

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: he shall go to the generation of his fathers. They shall never see the light.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man who has riches without understanding, is like the animals that perish.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A Psalm by Asaph. The Mighty One, God, Yahweh, speaks, and calls the earth from sunrise to sunset.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty, God shines out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Our God comes, and does not keep silent. A fire devours before him. It is very stormy around him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He calls to the heavens above, to the earth, that he may judge his people:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Gather my saints together to me, those who have made a covenant with me by sacrifice.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The heavens shall declare his righteousness, for God himself is judge. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Hear, my people, and I will speak; Israel, and I will testify against you. I am God, your God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I don’t rebuke you for your sacrifices. Your burnt offerings are continually before me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have no need for a bull from your stall, nor male goats from your pens.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For every animal of the forest is mine, and the livestock on a thousand hills.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I know all the birds of the mountains. The wild animals of the field are mine.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If I were hungry, I would not tell you, for the world is mine, and all that is in it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will I eat the meat of bulls, or drink the blood of goats?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Offer to God the sacrifice of thanksgiving. Pay your vows to the Most High.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Call on me in the day of trouble. I will deliver you, and you will honor me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But to the wicked God says, “What right do you have to declare my statutes, that you have taken my covenant on your lips,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: since you hate instruction, and throw my words behind you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When you saw a thief, you consented with him, and have participated with adulterers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “You give your mouth to evil. Your tongue frames deceit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You sit and speak against your brother. You slander your own mother’s son.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have done these things, and I kept silent. You thought that I was just like you. I will rebuke you, and accuse you in front of your eyes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now consider this, you who forget God, lest I tear you into pieces, and there be no one to deliver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever offers the sacrifice of thanksgiving glorifies me, and prepares his way so that I will show God’s salvation to him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David, when Nathan the prophet came to him, after he had gone in to Bathsheba. Have mercy on me, God, according to your loving kindness. According to the multitude of your tender mercies, blot out my transgressions.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity. Cleanse me from my sin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I know my transgressions. My sin is constantly before me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Against you, and you only, I have sinned, and done that which is evil in your sight; that you may be proved right when you speak, and justified when you judge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, I was born in iniquity. In sin my mother conceived me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, you desire truth in the inward parts. You teach me wisdom in the inmost place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Purify me with hyssop, and I will be clean. Wash me, and I will be whiter than snow.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let me hear joy and gladness, That the bones which you have broken may rejoice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hide your face from my sins, and blot out all of my iniquities.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Create in me a clean heart, O God. Renew a right spirit within me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t throw me from your presence, and don’t take your holy Spirit from me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Restore to me the joy of your salvation. Uphold me with a willing spirit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then I will teach transgressors your ways. Sinners shall be converted to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Deliver me from the guilt of bloodshed, O God, the God of my salvation. My tongue shall sing aloud of your righteousness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Lord, open my lips. My mouth shall declare your praise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you don’t delight in sacrifice, or else I would give it. You have no pleasure in burnt offering.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit. A broken and contrite heart, O God, you will not despise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Do well in your good pleasure to Zion. Build the walls of Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then you will delight in the sacrifices of righteousness, in burnt offerings and in whole burnt offerings. Then they will offer bulls on your altar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. A contemplation by David, when Doeg the Edomite came and told Saul, “David has come to Ahimelech’s house.” Why do you boast of mischief, mighty man? God’s loving kindness endures continually.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your tongue plots destruction, like a sharp razor, working deceitfully.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You love evil more than good, lying rather than speaking the truth. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You love all devouring words, you deceitful tongue.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God will likewise destroy you forever. He will take you up, and pluck you out of your tent, and root you out of the land of the living. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The righteous also will see it, and fear, and laugh at him, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Behold, this is the man who didn’t make God his strength, but trusted in the abundance of his riches, and strengthened himself in his wickedness.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But as for me, I am like a green olive tree in God’s house. I trust in God’s loving kindness forever and ever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will give you thanks forever, because you have done it. I will hope in your name, for it is good, in the presence of your saints.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Mahalath.” A contemplation by David. The fool has said in his heart, “There is no God.” They are corrupt, and have done abominable iniquity. There is no one who does good.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God looks down from heaven on the children of men, to see if there are any who understood, who seek after God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Every one of them has gone back. They have become filthy together. There is no one who does good, no, not one.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Have the workers of iniquity no knowledge, who eat up my people as they eat bread, and don’t call on God?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There they were in great fear, where no fear was, for God has scattered the bones of him who encamps against you. You have put them to shame, because God has rejected them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Oh that the salvation of Israel would come out of Zion! When God brings back his people from captivity, then Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. On stringed instruments. A contemplation by David, when the Ziphites came and said to Saul, “Isn’t David hiding himself among us?” Save me, God, by your name. Vindicate me in your might.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hear my prayer, God. Listen to the words of my mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For strangers have risen up against me. Violent men have sought after my soul. They haven’t set God before them. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, God is my helper. The Lord is the one who sustains my soul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will repay the evil to my enemies. Destroy them in your truth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With a free will offering, I will sacrifice to you. I will give thanks to your name, Yahweh, for it is good.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he has delivered me out of all trouble. My eye has seen triumph over my enemies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. On stringed instruments. A contemplation by David. Listen to my prayer, God. Don’t hide yourself from my supplication.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Attend to me, and answer me. I am restless in my complaint, and moan,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Because of the voice of the enemy, Because of the oppression of the wicked. For they bring suffering on me. In anger they hold a grudge against me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My heart is severely pained within me. The terrors of death have fallen on me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Fearfulness and trembling have come on me. Horror has overwhelmed me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I said, “Oh that I had wings like a dove! Then I would fly away, and be at rest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, then I would wander far off. I would lodge in the wilderness.” Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I would hurry to a shelter from the stormy wind and storm.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Confuse them, Lord, and confound their language, for I have seen violence and strife in the city.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Day and night they prowl around on its walls. Malice and abuse are also within her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Destructive forces are within her. Threats and lies don’t depart from her streets.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For it was not an enemy who insulted me, then I could have endured it. Neither was it he who hated me who raised himself up against me, then I would have hidden myself from him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But it was you, a man like me, my companion, and my familiar friend.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: We took sweet fellowship together. We walked in God’s house with company.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let death come suddenly on them. Let them go down alive into Sheol.  For wickedness is among them, in their dwelling.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As for me, I will call on God. Yahweh will save me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Evening, morning, and at noon, I will cry out in distress. He will hear my voice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has redeemed my soul in peace from the battle that was against me, although there are many who oppose me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God, who is enthroned forever, will hear, and answer them. Selah. They never change, who don’t fear God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He raises his hands against his friends. He has violated his covenant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His mouth was smooth as butter, but his heart was war. His words were softer than oil, yet they were drawn swords.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Cast your burden on Yahweh, and he will sustain you. He will never allow the righteous to be moved.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But you, God, will bring them down into the pit of destruction. Bloodthirsty and deceitful men shall not live out half their days, but I will trust in you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Silent Dove in Distant Lands.” A poem by David, when the Philistines seized him in Gath. Be merciful to me, God, for man wants to swallow me up. All day long, he attacks and oppresses me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My enemies want to swallow me up all day long, for they are many who fight proudly against me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When I am afraid, I will put my trust in you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In God, I praise his word. In God, I put my trust. I will not be afraid. What can flesh do to me?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All day long they twist my words. All their thoughts are against me for evil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They conspire and lurk, watching my steps, they are eager to take my life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Shall they escape by iniquity? In anger cast down the peoples, God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You count my wanderings. You put my tears into your bottle. Aren’t they in your book?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then my enemies shall turn back in the day that I call. I know this, that God is for me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In God, I will praise his word. In Yahweh, I will praise his word.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have put my trust in God. I will not be afraid. What can man do to me?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your vows are on me, God. I will give thank offerings to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you have delivered my soul from death, and prevented my feet from falling, that I may walk before God in the light of the living.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” A poem by David, when he fled from Saul, in the cave. Be merciful to me, God, be merciful to me, for my soul takes refuge in you. Yes, in the shadow of your wings, I will take refuge, until disaster has passed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I cry out to God Most High, to God who accomplishes my requests for me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will send from heaven, and save me, he rebukes the one who is pursuing me. Selah. God will send out his loving kindness and his truth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My soul is among lions. I lie among those who are set on fire, even the sons of men, whose teeth are spears and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Be exalted, God, above the heavens! Let your glory be above all the earth!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They have prepared a net for my steps. My soul is bowed down. They dig a pit before me. They fall into the middle of it themselves. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My heart is steadfast, God, my heart is steadfast. I will sing, yes, I will sing praises.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wake up, my glory! Wake up, lute and harp! I will wake up the dawn.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will give thanks to you, Lord, among the peoples. I will sing praises to you among the nations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For your great loving kindness reaches to the heavens, and your truth to the skies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Be exalted, God, above the heavens. Let your glory be over all the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” A poem by David. Do you indeed speak righteousness, silent ones? Do you judge blamelessly, you sons of men?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: No, in your heart you plot injustice. You measure out the violence of your hands in the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wicked go astray from the womb. They are wayward as soon as they are born, speaking lies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their poison is like the poison of a snake; like a deaf cobra that stops its ear,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: which doesn’t listen to the voice of charmers, no matter how skillful the charmer may be.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Break their teeth, God, in their mouth. Break out the great teeth of the young lions, Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let them vanish like water that flows away. When they draw the bow, let their arrows be made blunt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let them be like a snail which melts and passes away, like the stillborn child, who has not seen the sun.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Before your pots can feel the heat of the thorns, he will sweep away the green and the burning alike.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The righteous shall rejoice when he sees the vengeance. He shall wash his feet in the blood of the wicked;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so that men shall say, “Most certainly there is a reward for the righteous. Most certainly there is a God who judges the earth.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” A poem by David, when Saul sent, and they watched the house to kill him. Deliver me from my enemies, my God. Set me on high from those who rise up against me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Deliver me from the workers of iniquity. Save me from the bloodthirsty men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For, behold, they lie in wait for my soul. The mighty gather themselves together against me, not for my disobedience, nor for my sin, Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have done no wrong, yet they are ready to attack me. Rise up, behold, and help me!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You, Yahweh God of Armies, the God of Israel, rouse yourself to punish the nations. Show no mercy to the wicked traitors. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They return at evening, howling like dogs, and prowl around the city.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, they spew with their mouth. Swords are in their lips, “For”, they say, “who hears us?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But you, Yahweh, laugh at them. You scoff at all the nations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Oh, my Strength, I watch for you, for God is my high tower.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My God will go before me with his loving kindness. God will let me look at my enemies in triumph.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t kill them, or my people may forget. Scatter them by your power, and bring them down, Lord our shield.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the sin of their mouth, and the words of their lips, let them be caught in their pride, for the curses and lies which they utter.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Consume them in wrath. Consume them, and they will be no more. Let them know that God rules in Jacob, to the ends of the earth. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At evening let them return. Let them howl like a dog, and go around the city.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall wander up and down for food, and wait all night if they aren’t satisfied.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I will sing of your strength. Yes, I will sing aloud of your loving kindness in the morning. For you have been my high tower, a refuge in the day of my distress.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: To you, my strength, I will sing praises. For God is my high tower, the God of my mercy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “The Lily of the Covenant.” A teaching poem by David, when he fought with Aram Naharaim and with Aram Zobah, and Joab returned, and killed twelve thousand of Edom in the Valley of Salt. God, you have rejected us. You have broken us down. You have been angry. Restore us, again.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have made the land tremble. You have torn it. Mend its fractures, for it quakes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have shown your people hard things. You have made us drink the wine that makes us stagger.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have given a banner to those who fear you, that it may be displayed because of the truth. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So that your beloved may be delivered, save with your right hand, and answer us.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God has spoken from his sanctuary: “I will triumph. I will divide Shechem, and measure out the valley of Succoth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine. Ephraim also is the defense of my head. Judah is my scepter.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moab is my wash basin. I will throw my shoe on Edom. I shout in triumph over Philistia.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who will bring me into the strong city? Who has led me to Edom?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Haven’t you, God, rejected us? You don’t go out with our armies, God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Give us help against the adversary, for the help of man is vain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Through God we shall do valiantly, for it is he who will tread down our adversaries.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. For a stringed instrument. By David. Hear my cry, God. Listen to my prayer.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: From the end of the earth, I will call to you, when my heart is overwhelmed. Lead me to the rock that is higher than I.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you have been a refuge for me, a strong tower from the enemy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will dwell in your tent forever. I will take refuge in the shelter of your wings. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you, God, have heard my vows. You have given me the heritage of those who fear your name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You will prolong the king’s life; his years shall be for generations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall be enthroned in God’s presence forever. Appoint your loving kindness and truth, that they may preserve him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So I will sing praise to your name forever, that I may fulfill my vows daily.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. To Jeduthun. A Psalm by David. My soul rests in God alone. My salvation is from him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He alone is my rock and my salvation, my fortress — I will never be greatly shaken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How long will you assault a man, would all of you throw him down, Like a leaning wall, like a tottering fence?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They fully intend to throw him down from his lofty place. They delight in lies. They bless with their mouth, but they curse inwardly. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My soul, wait in silence for God alone, for my expectation is from him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He alone is my rock and my salvation, my fortress. I will not be shaken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With God is my salvation and my honor. The rock of my strength, and my refuge, is in God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Trust in him at all times, you people. Pour out your heart before him. God is a refuge for us. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Surely men of low degree are just a breath, and men of high degree are a lie. In the balances they will go up. They are together lighter than a breath.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t trust in oppression. Don’t become vain in robbery. If riches increase, don’t set your heart on them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God has spoken once; twice I have heard this, that power belongs to God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also to you, Lord, belongs loving kindness, for you reward every man according to his work.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A Psalm by David, when he was in the desert of Judah. God, you are my God. I will earnestly seek you. My soul thirsts for you. My flesh longs for you, in a dry and weary land, where there is no water.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So I have seen you in the sanctuary, watching your power and your glory.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Because your loving kindness is better than life, my lips shall praise you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So I will bless you while I live. I will lift up my hands in your name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My soul shall be satisfied as with the richest food. My mouth shall praise you with joyful lips,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when I remember you on my bed, and think about you in the night watches.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you have been my help. I will rejoice in the shadow of your wings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My soul stays close to you. Your right hand holds me up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But those who seek my soul, to destroy it, shall go into the lower parts of the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall be given over to the power of the sword. They shall be jackal food.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the king shall rejoice in God. Everyone who swears by him will praise him, for the mouth of those who speak lies shall be silenced.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. Hear my voice, God, in my complaint. Preserve my life from fear of the enemy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hide me from the conspiracy of the wicked, from the noisy crowd of the ones doing evil;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who sharpen their tongue like a sword, and aim their arrows, deadly words,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to shoot innocent men from ambushes. They shoot at him suddenly and fearlessly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They encourage themselves in evil plans. They talk about laying snares secretly. They say, “Who will see them?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They plot injustice, saying, “We have made a perfect plan!” Surely man’s mind and heart are cunning.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But God will shoot at them. They will be suddenly struck down with an arrow.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their own tongues shall ruin them. All who see them will shake their heads.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All mankind shall be afraid. They shall declare the work of God, and shall wisely ponder what he has done.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The righteous shall be glad in Yahweh, and shall take refuge in him. All the upright in heart shall praise him!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. A song. Praise waits for you, God, in Zion. To you shall vows be performed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You who hear prayer, to you all men will come.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sins overwhelmed me, but you atoned for our transgressions.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed is one whom you choose, and cause to come near, that he may live in your courts. We will be filled with the goodness of your house, your holy temple.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By awesome deeds of righteousness, you answer us, God of our salvation. You who are the hope of all the ends of the earth, of those who are far away on the sea;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who by his power forms the mountains, having armed yourself with strength;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who stills the roaring of the seas, the roaring of their waves, and the turmoil of the nations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They also who dwell in faraway places are afraid at your wonders. You call the morning’s dawn and the evening with songs of joy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You visit the earth, and water it. You greatly enrich it. The river of God is full of water. You provide them grain, for so you have ordained it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You drench its furrows. You level its ridges. You soften it with showers. You bless it with a crop.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You crown the year with your bounty. Your carts overflow with abundance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wilderness grasslands overflow. The hills are clothed with gladness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The pastures are covered with flocks. The valleys also are clothed with grain. They shout for joy! They also sing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. A song. A Psalm. Make a joyful shout to God, all the earth!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sing to the glory of his name! Offer glory and praise!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Tell God, “How awesome are your deeds! Through the greatness of your power, your enemies submit themselves to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the earth will worship you, and will sing to you; they will sing to your name.” Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Come, and see God’s deeds — awesome work on behalf of the children of men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He turned the sea into dry land. They went through the river on foot. There, we rejoiced in him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He rules by his might forever. His eyes watch the nations. Don’t let the rebellious rise up against him. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Praise our God, you peoples! Make the sound of his praise heard,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who preserves our life among the living, and doesn’t allow our feet to be moved.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you, God, have tested us. You have refined us, as silver is refined.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You brought us into prison. You laid a burden on our backs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You allowed men to ride over our heads. We went through fire and through water, but you brought us to the place of abundance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will come into your temple with burnt offerings. I will pay my vows to you,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: which my lips promised, and my mouth spoke, when I was in distress.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will offer to you burnt offerings of fat animals, with the offering of rams, I will offer bulls with goats. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Come, and hear, all you who fear God. I will declare what he has done for my soul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I cried to him with my mouth. He was extolled with my tongue.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If I cherished sin in my heart, the Lord wouldn’t have listened.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But most certainly, God has listened. He has heard the voice of my prayer.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed be God, who has not turned away my prayer, nor his loving kindness from me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. With stringed instruments. A Psalm. A song. May God be merciful to us, bless us, and cause his face to shine on us. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That your way may be known on earth, and your salvation among all nations,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: let the peoples praise you, God. Let all the peoples praise you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Oh let the nations be glad and sing for joy, for you will judge the peoples with equity, and govern the nations on earth. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let the peoples praise you, God. Let all the peoples praise you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The earth has yielded its increase. God, even our own God, will bless us.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God will bless us. All the ends of the earth shall fear him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. A song. Let God arise! Let his enemies be scattered! Let them who hate him also flee before him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As smoke is driven away, so drive them away. As wax melts before the fire, so let the wicked perish at the presence of God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But let the righteous be glad. Let them rejoice before God. Yes, let them rejoice with gladness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sing to God! Sing praises to his name! Extol him who rides on the clouds: to Yah, his name! Rejoice before him!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A father of the fatherless, and a defender of the widows, is God in his holy habitation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God sets the lonely in families. He brings out the prisoners with singing, but the rebellious dwell in a sun-scorched land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God, when you went out before your people, when you marched through the wilderness... Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The earth trembled. The sky also poured down rain at the presence of the God of Sinai — at the presence of God, the God of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You, God, sent a plentiful rain. You confirmed your inheritance, when it was weary.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your congregation lived therein. You, God, prepared your goodness for the poor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Lord announced the word. The ones who proclaim it are a great company.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Kings of armies flee! They flee!” She who waits at home divides the plunder,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: while you sleep among the camp fires, the wings of a dove sheathed with silver, her feathers with shining gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the Almighty scattered kings in her, it snowed on Zalmon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The mountains of Bashan are majestic mountains. The mountains of Bashan are rugged.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why do you look in envy, you rugged mountains, at the mountain where God chooses to reign? Yes, Yahweh will dwell there forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The chariots of God are tens of thousands and thousands of thousands. The Lord is among them, from Sinai, into the sanctuary.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have ascended on high. You have led away captives. You have received gifts among men, yes, among the rebellious also, that Yah God might dwell there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed be the Lord, who daily bears our burdens, even the God who is our salvation. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God is to us a God of deliverance. To Yahweh, the Lord, belongs escape from death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But God will strike through the head of his enemies, the hairy scalp of such a one as still continues in his guiltiness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Lord said, “I will bring you again from Bashan, I will bring you again from the depths of the sea;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That you may crush them, dipping your foot in blood, that the tongues of your dogs may have their portion from your enemies.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They have seen your processions, God, even the processions of my God, my King, into the sanctuary.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The singers went before, the minstrels followed after, among the ladies playing with tambourines,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Bless God in the congregations, even the Lord in the assembly of Israel!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is little Benjamin, their ruler, the princes of Judah, their council, the princes of Zebulun, and the princes of Naphtali.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your God has commanded your strength. Strengthen, God, that which you have done for us.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Because of your temple at Jerusalem, kings shall bring presents to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Rebuke the wild animal of the reeds, the multitude of the bulls, with the calves of the peoples. Being humbled, may it bring bars of silver. Scatter the nations that delight in war.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Princes shall come out of Egypt. Ethiopia shall hurry to stretch out her hands to God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sing to God, you kingdoms of the earth! Sing praises to the Lord! Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: To him who rides on the heaven of heavens, which are of old; behold, he utters his voice, a mighty voice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ascribe strength to God! His excellency is over Israel, his strength is in the skies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You are awesome, God, in your sanctuaries. The God of Israel gives strength and power to his people. Praise be to God!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Lilies.” By David. Save me, God, for the waters have come up to my neck!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I sink in deep mire, where there is no foothold. I have come into deep waters, where the floods overflow me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am weary with my crying. My throat is dry. My eyes fail, looking for my God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of my head. Those who want to cut me off, being my enemies wrongfully, are mighty. I have to restore what I didn’t take away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God, you know my foolishness. My sins aren’t hidden from you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t let those who wait for you be shamed through me, Lord Yahweh of Armies. Don’t let those who seek you be brought to dishonor through me, God of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Because for your sake, I have borne reproach. Shame has covered my face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have become a stranger to my brothers, an alien to my mother’s children.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the zeal of your house consumes me. The reproaches of those who reproach you have fallen on me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When I wept and I fasted, that was to my reproach.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When I made sackcloth my clothing, I became a byword to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who sit in the gate talk about me. I am the song of the drunkards.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But as for me, my prayer is to you, Yahweh, in an acceptable time. God, in the abundance of your loving kindness, answer me in the truth of your salvation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Deliver me out of the mire, and don’t let me sink. Let me be delivered from those who hate me, and out of the deep waters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t let the flood waters overwhelm me, neither let the deep swallow me up. Don’t let the pit shut its mouth on me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Answer me, Yahweh, for your loving kindness is good. According to the multitude of your tender mercies, turn to me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t hide your face from your servant, for I am in distress. Answer me speedily!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Draw near to my soul, and redeem it. Ransom me because of my enemies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You know my reproach, my shame, and my dishonor. My adversaries are all before you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Reproach has broken my heart, and I am full of heaviness. I looked for some to take pity, but there was none; for comforters, but I found none.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They also gave me gall for my food. In my thirst, they gave me vinegar to drink.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let their table before them become a snare. May it become a retribution and a trap.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let their eyes be darkened, so that they can’t see. Let their backs be continually bent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pour out your indignation on them. Let the fierceness of your anger overtake them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let their habitation be desolate. Let no one dwell in their tents.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they persecute him whom you have wounded. They tell of the sorrow of those whom you have hurt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Charge them with crime upon crime. Don’t let them come into your righteousness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let them be blotted out of the book of life, and not be written with the righteous.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I am in pain and distress. Let your salvation, God, protect me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will praise the name of God with a song, and will magnify him with thanksgiving.
