translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It will please Yahweh better than an ox, or a bull that has horns and hoofs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The humble have seen it, and are glad. You who seek after God, let your heart live.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Yahweh hears the needy, and doesn’t despise his captive people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let heaven and earth praise him; the seas, and everything that moves therein!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For God will save Zion, and build the cities of Judah. They shall settle there, and own it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children also of his servants shall inherit it. Those who love his name shall dwell therein.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. By David. A reminder. Hurry, God, to deliver me. Come quickly to help me, Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let them be disappointed and confounded who seek my soul. Let those who desire my ruin be turned back in disgrace.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let them be turned because of their shame Who say, “Aha! Aha!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let all those who seek you rejoice and be glad in you. Let those who love your salvation continually say, “Let God be exalted!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I am poor and needy. Come to me quickly, God. You are my help and my deliverer. Yahweh, don’t delay.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In you, Yahweh, I take refuge. Never let me be disappointed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Deliver me in your righteousness, and rescue me. Turn your ear to me, and save me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Be to me a rock of refuge to which I may always go. Give the command to save me, for you are my rock and my fortress.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Rescue me, my God, from the hand of the wicked, from the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you are my hope, Lord Yahweh; my confidence from my youth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have relied on you from the womb. You are he who took me out of my mother’s womb. I will always praise you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am a marvel to many, but you are my strong refuge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My mouth shall be filled with your praise, with your honor all day long.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t reject me in my old age. Don’t forsake me when my strength fails.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For my enemies talk about me. Those who watch for my soul conspire together,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: saying, “God has forsaken him. Pursue and take him, for no one will rescue him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God, don’t be far from me. My God, hurry to help me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let my accusers be disappointed and consumed. Let them be covered with disgrace and scorn who want to harm me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I will always hope, and will add to all of your praise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My mouth will tell about your righteousness, and of your salvation all day, though I don’t know its full measure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will come with the mighty acts of the Lord Yahweh. I will make mention of your righteousness, even of yours alone.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God, you have taught me from my youth. Until now, I have declared your wondrous works.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, even when I am old and gray-haired, God, don’t forsake me, until I have declared your strength to the next generation, your might to everyone who is to come.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your righteousness also, God, reaches to the heavens; you have done great things. God, who is like you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You, who have shown us many and bitter troubles, you will let me live. You will bring us up again from the depths of the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Increase my honor, and comfort me again.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will also praise you with the harp for your faithfulness, my God. I sing praises to you with the lyre, Holy One of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My lips shall shout for joy! My soul, which you have redeemed, sings praises to you!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My tongue will also talk about your righteousness all day long, for they are disappointed, and they are confounded, who want to harm me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By Solomon. God, give the king your justice; your righteousness to the royal son.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will judge your people with righteousness, and your poor with justice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The mountains shall bring prosperity to the people. The hills bring the fruit of righteousness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will judge the poor of the people. He will save the children of the needy, and will break the oppressor in pieces.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall fear you while the sun endures; and as long as the moon, throughout all generations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will come down like rain on the mown grass, as showers that water the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In his days, the righteous shall flourish, and abundance of peace, until the moon is no more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He shall have dominion also from sea to sea, from the River to the ends of the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who dwell in the wilderness shall bow before him. His enemies shall lick the dust.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The kings of Tarshish and of the islands will bring tribute. The kings of Sheba and Seba shall offer gifts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, all kings shall fall down before him. All nations shall serve him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he will deliver the needy when he cries; the poor, who has no helper.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will have pity on the poor and needy. He will save the souls of the needy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will redeem their soul from oppression and violence. Their blood will be precious in his sight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall live, and to him shall be given of the gold of Sheba. Men shall pray for him continually. They shall bless him all day long.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Abundance of grain shall be throughout the land. Its fruit sways like Lebanon. Let it flourish, thriving like the grass of the field.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His name endures forever. His name continues as long as the sun. Men shall be blessed by him. All nations will call him blessed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Praise be to Yahweh God, the God of Israel, who alone does marvelous deeds.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed be his glorious name forever! Let the whole earth be filled with his glory! Amen and amen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This ends the prayers by David, the son of Jesse.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A Psalm by Asaph. Surely God is good to Israel, to those who are pure in heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But as for me, my feet were almost gone. My steps had nearly slipped.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I was envious of the arrogant, when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For there are no struggles in their death, but their strength is firm.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They are free from burdens of men, neither are they plagued like other men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore pride is like a chain around their neck. Violence covers them like a garment.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their eyes bulge with fat. Their minds pass the limits of conceit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They scoff and speak with malice. In arrogance, they threaten oppression.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They have set their mouth in the heavens. Their tongue walks through the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore their people return to them, and they drink up waters of abundance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They say, “How does God know? Is there knowledge in the Most High?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, these are the wicked. Being always at ease, they increase in riches.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Surely I have cleansed my heart in vain, and washed my hands in innocence,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For all day long I have been plagued, and punished every morning.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If I had said, “I will speak thus”; behold, I would have betrayed the generation of your children.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When I tried to understand this, it was too painful for me;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Until I entered God’s sanctuary, and considered their latter end.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Surely you set them in slippery places. You throw them down to destruction.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How they are suddenly destroyed! They are completely swept away with terrors.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As a dream when one wakes up, so, Lord,  when you awake, you will despise their fantasies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For my soul was grieved. I was embittered in my heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I was so senseless and ignorant. I was a brute beast before you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless, I am continually with you. You have held my right hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You will guide me with your counsel, and afterward receive me to glory.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whom do I have in heaven? There is no one on earth whom I desire besides you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My flesh and my heart fails, but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For, behold, those who are far from you shall perish. You have destroyed all those who are unfaithful to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But it is good for me to come close to God. I have made the Lord Yahweh  my refuge, that I may tell of all your works.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A contemplation by Asaph. God, why have you rejected us forever? Why does your anger smolder against the sheep of your pasture?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Remember your congregation, which you purchased of old, which you have redeemed to be the tribe of your inheritance; Mount Zion, in which you have lived.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Lift up your feet to the perpetual ruins, all the evil that the enemy has done in the sanctuary.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your adversaries have roared in the middle of your assembly. They have set up their standards as signs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They behaved like men wielding axes, cutting through a thicket of trees.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now they break all its carved work down with hatchet and hammers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They have burned your sanctuary to the ground. They have profaned the dwelling place of your Name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said in their heart, “We will crush them completely.” They have burned up all the places in the land where God was worshiped.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: We see no miraculous signs. There is no longer any prophet, neither is there among us anyone who knows how long.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How long, God, shall the adversary reproach? Shall the enemy blaspheme your name forever?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why do you draw back your hand, even your right hand? Take it out of your pocket and consume them!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet God is my King of old, working salvation throughout the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You divided the sea by your strength. You broke the heads of the sea monsters in the waters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You broke the heads of Leviathan in pieces. You gave him as food to people and desert creatures.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You opened up spring and stream. You dried up mighty rivers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The day is yours, the night is also yours. You have prepared the light and the sun.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have set all the boundaries of the earth. You have made summer and winter.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Remember this, that the enemy has mocked you, Yahweh. Foolish people have blasphemed your name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t deliver the soul of your dove to wild beasts. Don’t forget the life of your poor forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Honor your covenant, for haunts of violence fill the dark places of the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t let the oppressed return ashamed. Let the poor and needy praise your name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Arise, God! Plead your own cause. Remember how the foolish man mocks you all day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t forget the voice of your adversaries. The tumult of those who rise up against you ascends continually.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” A Psalm by Asaph. A song. We give thanks to you, God. We give thanks, for your Name is near. Men tell about your wondrous works.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When I choose the appointed time, I will judge blamelessly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The earth and all its inhabitants quake. I firmly hold its pillars. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I said to the arrogant, “Don’t boast!” I said to the wicked, “Don’t lift up the horn.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t lift up your horn on high. Don’t speak with a stiff neck.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For neither from the east, nor from the west, nor yet from the south, comes exaltation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But God is the judge. He puts down one, and lifts up another.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For in Yahweh’s hand there is a cup, full of foaming wine mixed with spices. He pours it out. Indeed the wicked of the earth drink and drink it to its very dregs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I will declare this forever: I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will cut off all the horns of the wicked, but the horns of the righteous shall be lifted up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. On stringed instruments. A Psalm by Asaph. A song. In Judah, God is known. His name is great in Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His tabernacle is also in Salem; His dwelling place in Zion.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There he broke the flaming arrows of the bow, the shield, and the sword, and the weapons of war. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Glorious are you, and excellent, more than mountains of game.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Valiant men lie plundered, they have slept their last sleep. None of the men of war can lift their hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At your rebuke, God of Jacob, both chariot and horse are cast into a dead sleep.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You, even you, are to be feared. Who can stand in your sight when you are angry?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You pronounced judgment from heaven. The earth feared, and was silent,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when God arose to judgment, to save all the afflicted ones of the earth. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Surely the wrath of man praises you. The survivors of your wrath are restrained.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Make vows to Yahweh your God, and fulfill them! Let all of his neighbors bring presents to him who is to be feared.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will cut off the spirit of princes. He is feared by the kings of the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. To Jeduthun. A Psalm by Asaph. My cry goes to God! Indeed, I cry to God for help, and for him to listen to me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord. My hand was stretched out in the night, and didn’t get tired. My soul refused to be comforted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I remember God, and I groan. I complain, and my spirit is overwhelmed. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You hold my eyelids open. I am so troubled that I can’t speak.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have considered the days of old, the years of ancient times.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I remember my song in the night. I consider in my own heart; my spirit diligently inquires:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Will the Lord reject us forever? Will he be favorable no more?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Has his loving kindness vanished forever? Does his promise fail for generations?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Has God forgotten to be gracious? Has he, in anger, withheld his compassion?” Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then I thought, “I will appeal to this: the years of the right hand of the Most High.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will remember Yah’s deeds; for I will remember your wonders of old.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will also meditate on all your work, and consider your doings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your way, God, is in the sanctuary. What god is great like God?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You are the God who does wonders. You have made your strength known among the peoples.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have redeemed your people with your arm, the sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The waters saw you, God. The waters saw you, and they writhed. The depths also convulsed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The clouds poured out water. The skies resounded with thunder. Your arrows also flashed around.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The voice of your thunder was in the whirlwind. The lightnings lit up the world. The earth trembled and shook.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your way was through the sea; your paths through the great waters. Your footsteps were not known.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You led your people like a flock, by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A contemplation by Asaph. Hear my teaching, my people. Turn your ears to the words of my mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will open my mouth in a parable. I will utter dark sayings of old,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Which we have heard and known, and our fathers have told us.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: We will not hide them from their children, telling to the generation to come the praises of Yahweh, his strength, and his wondrous deeds that he has done.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he established a testimony in Jacob, and appointed a teaching in Israel, which he commanded our fathers, that they should make them known to their children;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that the generation to come might know, even the children who should be born; who should arise and tell their children,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that they might set their hope in God, and not forget God’s deeds, but keep his commandments,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and might not be as their fathers, a stubborn and rebellious generation, a generation that didn’t make their hearts loyal, whose spirit was not steadfast with God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Ephraim, being armed and carrying bows, turned back in the day of battle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They didn’t keep God’s covenant, and refused to walk in his law.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They forgot his doings, his wondrous deeds that he had shown them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He did marvelous things in the sight of their fathers, in the land of Egypt, in the field of Zoan.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He split the sea, and caused them to pass through. He made the waters stand as a heap.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the daytime he also led them with a cloud, and all night with a light of fire.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He split rocks in the wilderness, and gave them drink abundantly as out of the depths.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He brought streams also out of the rock, and caused waters to run down like rivers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet they still went on to sin against him, to rebel against the Most High in the desert.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They tempted God in their heart by asking food according to their desire.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, they spoke against God. They said, “Can God prepare a table in the wilderness?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, he struck the rock, so that waters gushed out, and streams overflowed. Can he give bread also? Will he provide meat for his people?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore Yahweh heard, and was angry. A fire was kindled against Jacob, anger also went up against Israel,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: because they didn’t believe in God, and didn’t trust in his salvation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet he commanded the skies above, and opened the doors of heaven.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He rained down manna on them to eat, and gave them food from the sky.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Man ate the bread of angels. He sent them food to the full.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He caused the east wind to blow in the sky. By his power he guided the south wind.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He rained also meat on them as the dust; winged birds as the sand of the seas.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He let them fall in the middle of their camp, around their habitations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So they ate, and were well filled. He gave them their own desire.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They didn’t turn from their cravings. Their food was yet in their mouths,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when the anger of God went up against them, killed some of their fattest, and struck down the young men of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For all this they still sinned, and didn’t believe in his wondrous works.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore he consumed their days in vanity, and their years in terror.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he killed them, then they inquired after him. They returned and sought God earnestly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They remembered that God was their rock, the Most High God, their redeemer.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But they flattered him with their mouth, and lied to him with their tongue.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For their heart was not right with him, neither were they faithful in his covenant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he, being merciful, forgave iniquity, and didn’t destroy them. Yes, many times he turned his anger away, and didn’t stir up all his wrath.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He remembered that they were but flesh, a wind that passes away, and doesn’t come again.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness, and grieved him in the desert!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They turned again and tempted God, and provoked the Holy One of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They didn’t remember his hand, nor the day when he redeemed them from the adversary;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: how he set his signs in Egypt, his wonders in the field of Zoan,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: he turned their rivers into blood, and their streams, so that they could not drink.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sent among them swarms of flies, which devoured them; and frogs, which destroyed them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He gave also their increase to the caterpillar, and their labor to the locust.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He destroyed their vines with hail, their sycamore fig trees with frost.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He gave over their livestock also to the hail, and their flocks to hot thunderbolts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He threw on them the fierceness of his anger, wrath, indignation, and trouble, and a band of angels of evil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made a path for his anger. He didn’t spare their soul from death, but gave their life over to the pestilence,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and struck all the firstborn in Egypt, the chief of their strength in the tents of Ham.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he led out his own people like sheep, and guided them in the wilderness like a flock.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He led them safely, so that they weren’t afraid, but the sea overwhelmed their enemies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He brought them to the border of his sanctuary, to this mountain, which his right hand had taken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also drove out the nations before them, allotted them for an inheritance by line, and made the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tents.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet they tempted and rebelled against the Most High God, and didn’t keep his testimonies;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but turned back, and dealt treacherously like their fathers. They were turned aside like a deceitful bow.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they provoked him to anger with their high places, and moved him to jealousy with their engraved images.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When God heard this, he was angry, and greatly abhorred Israel;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So that he abandoned the tent of Shiloh, the tent which he placed among men;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and delivered his strength into captivity, his glory into the adversary’s hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also gave his people over to the sword, and was angry with his inheritance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Fire devoured their young men. Their virgins had no wedding song.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their priests fell by the sword, and their widows couldn’t weep.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the Lord awakened as one out of sleep, like a mighty man who shouts by reason of wine.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He struck his adversaries backward. He put them to a perpetual reproach.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover he rejected the tent of Joseph, and didn’t choose the tribe of Ephraim,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion which he loved.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He built his sanctuary like the heights, like the earth which he has established forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also chose David his servant, and took him from the sheepfolds;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: from following the ewes that have their young, he brought him to be the shepherd of Jacob, his people, and Israel, his inheritance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So he was their shepherd according to the integrity of his heart, and guided them by the skillfulness of his hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A Psalm by Asaph. God, the nations have come into your inheritance. They have defiled your holy temple. They have laid Jerusalem in heaps.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They have given the dead bodies of your servants to be food for the birds of the sky, the flesh of your saints to the animals of the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their blood they have shed like water around Jerusalem. There was no one to bury them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: We have become a reproach to our neighbors, a scoffing and derision to those who are around us.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How long, Yahweh? Will you be angry forever? Will your jealousy burn like fire?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pour out your wrath on the nations that don’t know you; on the kingdoms that don’t call on your name;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they have devoured Jacob, and destroyed his homeland.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t hold the iniquities of our forefathers against us. Let your tender mercies speedily meet us, for we are in desperate need.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Help us, God of our salvation, for the glory of your name. Deliver us, and forgive our sins, for your name’s sake.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why should the nations say, “Where is their God?” Let it be known among the nations, before our eyes, that vengeance for your servants’ blood is being poured out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let the sighing of the prisoner come before you. According to the greatness of your power, preserve those who are sentenced to death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pay back to our neighbors seven times into their bosom their reproach with which they have reproached you, Lord.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So we, your people and sheep of your pasture, will give you thanks forever. We will praise you forever, to all generations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “The Lilies of the Covenant.” A Psalm by Asaph. Hear us, Shepherd of Israel, you who lead Joseph like a flock, you who sit above the cherubim, shine out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh, stir up your might! Come to save us!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Turn us again, God. Cause your face to shine, and we will be saved.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh God of Armies, How long will you be angry against the prayer of your people?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have fed them with the bread of tears, and given them tears to drink in large measure.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You make us a source of contention to our neighbors. Our enemies laugh among themselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Turn us again, God of Armies. Cause your face to shine, and we will be saved.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You brought a vine out of Egypt. You drove out the nations, and planted it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You cleared the ground for it. It took deep root, and filled the land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The mountains were covered with its shadow. Its boughs were like God’s cedars.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It sent out its branches to the sea, Its shoots to the River.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why have you broken down its walls, so that all those who pass by the way pluck it?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The boar out of the wood ravages it. The wild animals of the field feed on it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Turn again, we beg you, God of Armies. Look down from heaven, and see, and visit this vine,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the stock which your right hand planted, the branch that you made strong for yourself.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It’s burned with fire. It’s cut down. They perish at your rebuke.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let your hand be on the man of your right hand, on the son of man whom you made strong for yourself.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So we will not turn away from you. Revive us, and we will call on your name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Turn us again, Yahweh God of Armies. Cause your face to shine, and we will be saved.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. On an instrument of Gath. By Asaph. Sing aloud to God, our strength! Make a joyful shout to the God of Jacob!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Raise a song, and bring here the tambourine, the pleasant lyre with the harp.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blow the trumpet at the New Moon, at the full moon, on our feast day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For it is a statute for Israel, an ordinance of the God of Jacob.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He appointed it in Joseph for a testimony, when he went out over the land of Egypt, I heard a language that I didn’t know.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I removed his shoulder from the burden. His hands were freed from the basket.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You called in trouble, and I delivered you. I answered you in the secret place of thunder. I tested you at the waters of Meribah.” Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Hear, my people, and I will testify to you, Israel, if you would listen to me!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There shall be no strange god in you, neither shall you worship any foreign god.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am Yahweh, your God, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt. Open your mouth wide, and I will fill it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But my people didn’t listen to my voice. Israel desired none of me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So I let them go after the stubbornness of their hearts, that they might walk in their own counsels.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Oh that my people would listen to me, that Israel would walk in my ways!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I would soon subdue their enemies, and turn my hand against their adversaries.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The haters of Yahweh would cringe before him, and their punishment would last forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he would have also fed them with the finest of the wheat. I will satisfy you with honey out of the rock.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A Psalm by Asaph. God presides in the great assembly. He judges among the gods.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “How long will you judge unjustly, and show partiality to the wicked?” Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Defend the weak, the poor, and the fatherless. Maintain the rights of the poor and oppressed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Rescue the weak and needy. Deliver them out of the hand of the wicked.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They don’t know, neither do they understand. They walk back and forth in darkness. All the foundations of the earth are shaken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I said, “You are gods, all of you are sons of the Most High.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless you shall die like men, and fall like one of the rulers.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Arise, God, judge the earth, for you inherit all of the nations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A song. A Psalm by Asaph. God, don’t keep silent. Don’t keep silent, and don’t be still, God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For, behold, your enemies are stirred up. Those who hate you have lifted up their heads.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They conspire with cunning against your people. They plot against your cherished ones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Come,” they say, “let’s destroy them as a nation, that the name of Israel may be remembered no more.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they have conspired together with one mind. They form an alliance against you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites; Moab, and the Hagrites;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Gebal, Ammon, and Amalek; Philistia with the inhabitants of Tyre;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Assyria also is joined with them. They have helped the children of Lot. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Do to them as you did to Midian, as to Sisera, as to Jabin, at the river Kishon;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who perished at Endor, who became as dung for the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb; yes, all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who said, “Let us take possession of God’s pasture lands.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My God, make them like tumbleweed; like chaff before the wind.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As the fire that burns the forest, as the flame that sets the mountains on fire,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so pursue them with your tempest, and terrify them with your storm.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Fill their faces with confusion, that they may seek your name, Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let them be disappointed and dismayed forever. Yes, let them be confounded and perish;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that they may know that you alone, whose name is Yahweh, are the Most High over all the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. On an instrument of Gath. A Psalm by the sons of Korah. How lovely are your dwellings, Yahweh of Armies!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My soul longs, and even faints for the courts of Yahweh. My heart and my flesh cry out for the living God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, the sparrow has found a home, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she may have her young, near your altars, Yahweh of Armies, my King, and my God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed are those who dwell in your house. They are always praising you. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed are those whose strength is in you; who have set their hearts on a pilgrimage.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Passing through the valley of Weeping, they make it a place of springs. Yes, the autumn rain covers it with blessings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They go from strength to strength. Everyone of them appears before God in Zion.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh, God of Armies, hear my prayer. Listen, God of Jacob. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, God our shield, look at the face of your anointed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For a day in your courts is better than a thousand. I would rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God, than to dwell in the tents of wickedness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Yahweh God is a sun and a shield. Yahweh will give grace and glory. He withholds no good thing from those who walk blamelessly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh of Armies, blessed is the man who trusts in you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by the sons of Korah. Yahweh, you have been favorable to your land. You have restored the fortunes of Jacob.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have forgiven the iniquity of your people. You have covered all their sin. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have taken away all your wrath. You have turned from the fierceness of your anger.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Turn us, God of our salvation, and cause your indignation toward us to cease.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Will you be angry with us forever? Will you draw out your anger to all generations?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Won’t you revive us again, that your people may rejoice in you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Show us your loving kindness, Yahweh. Grant us your salvation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will hear what God, Yahweh, will speak, for he will speak peace to his people, his saints; but let them not turn again to folly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Surely his salvation is near those who fear him, that glory may dwell in our land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Mercy and truth meet together. Righteousness and peace have kissed each other.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Truth springs out of the earth. Righteousness has looked down from heaven.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, Yahweh will give that which is good. Our land will yield its increase.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Righteousness goes before him, And prepares the way for his steps.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A Prayer by David. Hear, Yahweh, and answer me, for I am poor and needy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Preserve my soul, for I am godly. You, my God, save your servant who trusts in you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Be merciful to me, Lord, for I call to you all day long.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Bring joy to the soul of your servant, for to you, Lord, do I lift up my soul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you, Lord, are good, and ready to forgive; abundant in loving kindness to all those who call on you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hear, Yahweh, my prayer. Listen to the voice of my petitions.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the day of my trouble I will call on you, for you will answer me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is no one like you among the gods, Lord, nor any deeds like your deeds.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All nations you have made will come and worship before you, Lord. They shall glorify your name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you are great, and do wondrous things. You are God alone.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Teach me your way, Yahweh. I will walk in your truth. Make my heart undivided to fear your name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will praise you, Lord my God, with my whole heart. I will glorify your name forever more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For your loving kindness is great toward me. You have delivered my soul from the lowest Sheol.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God, the proud have risen up against me. A company of violent men have sought after my soul, and they don’t hold regard for you before them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But you, Lord, are a merciful and gracious God, slow to anger, and abundant in loving kindness and truth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Turn to me, and have mercy on me! Give your strength to your servant. Save the son of your servant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Show me a sign of your goodness, that those who hate me may see it, and be shamed, because you, Yahweh, have helped me, and comforted me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A Psalm by the sons of Korah; a Song. His foundation is in the holy mountains.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh loves the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Glorious things are spoken about you, city of God. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will record Rahab  and Babylon among those who acknowledge me. Behold, Philistia, Tyre, and also Ethiopia: “This one was born there.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, of Zion it will be said, “This one and that one was born in her”; the Most High himself will establish her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh will count, when he writes up the peoples, “This one was born there.” Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who sing as well as those who dance say, “All my springs are in you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A Song. A Psalm by the sons of Korah. For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “The Suffering of Affliction.” A contemplation by Heman, the Ezrahite. Yahweh, the God of my salvation, I have cried day and night before you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let my prayer enter into your presence. Turn your ear to my cry.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For my soul is full of troubles. My life draws near to Sheol.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am counted among those who go down into the pit. I am like a man who has no help,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: set apart among the dead, like the slain who lie in the grave, whom you remember no more. They are cut off from your hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have laid me in the lowest pit, in the darkest depths.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your wrath lies heavily on me. You have afflicted me with all your waves. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have taken my friends from me. You have made me an abomination to them. I am confined, and I can’t escape.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My eyes are dim from grief. I have called on you daily, Yahweh. I have spread out my hands to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Do you show wonders to the dead? Do the departed spirits rise up and praise you? Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Is your loving kindness declared in the grave? Or your faithfulness in Destruction?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Are your wonders made known in the dark? Or your righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But to you, Yahweh, I have cried. In the morning, my prayer comes before you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh, why do you reject my soul? Why do you hide your face from me?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am afflicted and ready to die from my youth up. While I suffer your terrors, I am distracted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your fierce wrath has gone over me. Your terrors have cut me off.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came around me like water all day long. They completely engulfed me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have put lover and friend far from me, and my friends into darkness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A contemplation by Ethan, the Ezrahite. I will sing of the loving kindness of Yahweh forever. With my mouth, I will make known your faithfulness to all generations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I indeed declare, “Love stands firm forever. You established the heavens. Your faithfulness is in them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I have made a covenant with my chosen one, I have sworn to David, my servant,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘I will establish your offspring forever, and build up your throne to all generations.’” Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The heavens will praise your wonders, Yahweh; your faithfulness also in the assembly of the holy ones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For who in the skies can be compared to Yahweh? Who among the sons of the heavenly beings is like Yahweh,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: a very awesome God in the council of the holy ones, to be feared above all those who are around him?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh, God of Armies, who is a mighty one, like you? Yah, your faithfulness is around you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You rule the pride of the sea. When its waves rise up, you calm them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have broken Rahab in pieces, like one of the slain. You have scattered your enemies with your mighty arm.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The heavens are yours. The earth also is yours; the world and its fullness. You have founded them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The north and the south, you have created them. Tabor and Hermon rejoice in your name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have a mighty arm. Your hand is strong, and your right hand is exalted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Righteousness and justice are the foundation of your throne. Loving kindness and truth go before your face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed are the people who learn to acclaim you. They walk in the light of your presence, Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In your name they rejoice all day. In your righteousness, they are exalted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you are the glory of their strength. In your favor, our horn will be exalted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For our shield belongs to Yahweh; our king to the Holy One of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then you spoke in vision to your saints, and said, “I have given strength to the warrior. I have exalted a young man from the people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have found David, my servant. I have anointed him with my holy oil,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: with whom my hand shall be established. My arm will also strengthen him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: No enemy will tax him. No wicked man will oppress him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will beat down his adversaries before him, and strike those who hate him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But my faithfulness and my loving kindness will be with him. In my name, his horn will be exalted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will set his hand also on the sea, and his right hand on the rivers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will call to me, ‘You are my Father, my God, and the rock of my salvation!’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will also appoint him my firstborn, the highest of the kings of the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will keep my loving kindness for him forever more. My covenant will stand firm with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will also make his offspring  endure forever, and his throne as the days of heaven.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If his children forsake my law, and don’t walk in my ordinances;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: if they break my statutes, and don’t keep my commandments;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then I will punish their sin with the rod, and their iniquity with stripes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I will not completely take my loving kindness from him, nor allow my faithfulness to fail.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will not break my covenant, nor alter what my lips have uttered.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Once I have sworn by my holiness, I will not lie to David.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His offspring  will endure forever, his throne like the sun before me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It will be established forever like the moon, the faithful witness in the sky.” Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But you have rejected and spurned. You have been angry with your anointed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have renounced the covenant of your servant. You have defiled his crown in the dust.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have broken down all his hedges. You have brought his strongholds to ruin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All who pass by the way rob him. He has become a reproach to his neighbors.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have exalted the right hand of his adversaries. You have made all of his enemies rejoice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, you turn back the edge of his sword, and haven’t supported him in battle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have ended his splendor, and thrown his throne down to the ground.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have shortened the days of his youth. You have covered him with shame. Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How long, Yahweh? Will you hide yourself forever? Will your wrath burn like fire?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Remember how short my time is! For what vanity have you created all the children of men!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What man is he who shall live and not see death, who shall deliver his soul from the power of Sheol? Selah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Lord, where are your former loving kindnesses, which you swore to David in your faithfulness?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Remember, Lord, the reproach of your servants, how I bear in my heart the taunts of all the mighty peoples,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With which your enemies have mocked, Yahweh, with which they have mocked the footsteps of your anointed one.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed be Yahweh forever more. Amen, and Amen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A Prayer by Moses, the man of God. Lord, you have been our dwelling place for all generations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Before the mountains were born, before you had formed the earth and the world, even from everlasting to everlasting, you are God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You turn man to destruction, saying, “Return, you children of men.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For a thousand years in your sight are just like yesterday when it is past, like a watch in the night.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You sweep them away as they sleep. In the morning they sprout like new grass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the morning it sprouts and springs up. By evening, it is withered and dry.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For we are consumed in your anger. We are troubled in your wrath.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have set our iniquities before you, our secret sins in the light of your presence.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For all our days have passed away in your wrath. We bring our years to an end as a sigh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The days of our years are seventy, or even by reason of strength eighty years; yet their pride is but labor and sorrow, for it passes quickly, and we fly away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who knows the power of your anger, your wrath according to the fear that is due to you?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So teach us to count our days, that we may gain a heart of wisdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Relent, Yahweh!  How long? Have compassion on your servants!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Satisfy us in the morning with your loving kindness, that we may rejoice and be glad all our days.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Make us glad for as many days as you have afflicted us, for as many years as we have seen evil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let your work appear to your servants; your glory to their children.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let the favor of the Lord our God be on us; establish the work of our hands for us; yes, establish the work of our hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who dwells in the secret place of the Most High will rest in the shadow of the Almighty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will say of Yahweh, “He is my refuge and my fortress; my God, in whom I trust.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he will deliver you from the snare of the fowler, and from the deadly pestilence.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will cover you with his feathers. Under his wings you will take refuge. His faithfulness is your shield and rampart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall not be afraid of the terror by night, nor of the arrow that flies by day;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: nor of the pestilence that walks in darkness, nor of the destruction that wastes at noonday.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A thousand may fall at your side, and ten thousand at your right hand; but it will not come near you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You will only look with your eyes, and see the recompense of the wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Because you have made Yahweh your refuge, and the Most High your dwelling place,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: no evil shall happen to you, neither shall any plague come near your dwelling.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he will put his angels in charge of you, to guard you in all your ways.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They will bear you up in their hands, so that you won’t dash your foot against a stone.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You will tread on the lion and cobra. You will trample the young lion and the serpent underfoot.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Because he has set his love on me, therefore I will deliver him. I will set him on high, because he has known my name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will call on me, and I will answer him. I will be with him in trouble. I will deliver him, and honor him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will satisfy him with long life, and show him my salvation.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A Psalm. A song for the Sabbath day. It is a good thing to give thanks to Yahweh, to sing praises to your name, Most High;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to proclaim your loving kindness in the morning, and your faithfulness every night,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: with the ten-stringed lute, with the harp, and with the melody of the lyre.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you, Yahweh, have made me glad through your work. I will triumph in the works of your hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How great are your works, Yahweh! Your thoughts are very deep.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A senseless man doesn’t know, neither does a fool understand this:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: though the wicked spring up as the grass, and all the evildoers flourish, they will be destroyed forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But you, Yahweh, are on high forever more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For, behold, your enemies, Yahweh, for, behold, your enemies shall perish. All the evildoers will be scattered.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But you have exalted my horn like that of the wild ox. I am anointed with fresh oil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My eye has also seen my enemies. My ears have heard of the wicked enemies who rise up against me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The righteous shall flourish like the palm tree. He will grow like a cedar in Lebanon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They are planted in Yahweh’s house. They will flourish in our God’s courts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They will still produce fruit in old age. They will be full of sap and green,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to show that Yahweh is upright. He is my rock, and there is no unrighteousness in him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh reigns! He is clothed with majesty! Yahweh is armed with strength. The world also is established. It can’t be moved.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your throne is established from long ago. You are from everlasting.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The floods have lifted up, Yahweh, the floods have lifted up their voice. The floods lift up their waves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Above the voices of many waters, the mighty breakers of the sea, Yahweh on high is mighty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your statutes stand firm. Holiness adorns your house, Yahweh, forever more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh, you God to whom vengeance belongs, you God to whom vengeance belongs, shine out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Rise up, you judge of the earth. Pay back the proud what they deserve.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh, how long will the wicked, how long will the wicked triumph?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They pour out arrogant words. All the evildoers boast.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They break your people in pieces, Yahweh, and afflict your heritage.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They kill the widow and the alien, and murder the fatherless.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They say, “Yah will not see, neither will Jacob’s God consider.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Consider, you senseless among the people; you fools, when will you be wise?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who implanted the ear, won’t he hear? He who formed the eye, won’t he see?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who disciplines the nations, won’t he punish? He who teaches man knows.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh knows the thoughts of man, that they are futile.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the man whom you discipline, Yah, and teach out of your law;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that you may give him rest from the days of adversity, until the pit is dug for the wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Yahweh won’t reject his people, neither will he forsake his inheritance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For judgment will return to righteousness. All the upright in heart shall follow it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who will rise up for me against the wicked? Who will stand up for me against the evildoers?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Unless Yahweh had been my help, my soul would have soon lived in silence.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When I said, “My foot is slipping!” Your loving kindness, Yahweh, held me up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the multitude of my thoughts within me, your comforts delight my soul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Shall the throne of wickedness have fellowship with you, which brings about mischief by statute?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They gather themselves together against the soul of the righteous, and condemn the innocent blood.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Yahweh has been my high tower, my God, the rock of my refuge.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has brought on them their own iniquity, and will cut them off in their own wickedness. Yahweh, our God, will cut them off.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Oh come, let’s sing to Yahweh. Let’s shout aloud to the rock of our salvation!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let’s come before his presence with thanksgiving. Let’s extol him with songs!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Yahweh is a great God, a great King above all gods.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In his hand are the deep places of the earth. The heights of the mountains are also his.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sea is his, and he made it. His hands formed the dry land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Oh come, let’s worship and bow down. Let’s kneel before Yahweh, our Maker,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for he is our God. We are the people of his pasture, and the sheep in his care. Today, oh that you would hear his voice!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t harden your heart, as at Meribah, as in the day of Massah in the wilderness,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when your fathers tempted me, tested me, and saw my work.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Forty long years I was grieved with that generation, and said, “It is a people that errs in their heart. They have not known my ways.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore I swore in my wrath, “They won’t enter into my rest.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sing to Yahweh a new song! Sing to Yahweh, all the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sing to Yahweh! Bless his name! Proclaim his salvation from day to day!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Declare his glory among the nations, his marvelous works among all the peoples.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For great is Yahweh, and greatly to be praised! He is to be feared above all gods.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For all the gods of the peoples are idols, but Yahweh made the heavens.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Honor and majesty are before him. Strength and beauty are in his sanctuary.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ascribe to Yahweh, you families of nations, ascribe to Yahweh glory and strength.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ascribe to Yahweh the glory due to his name. Bring an offering, and come into his courts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Worship Yahweh in holy array. Tremble before him, all the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Say among the nations, “Yahweh reigns.” The world is also established. It can’t be moved. He will judge the peoples with equity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let the heavens be glad, and let the earth rejoice. Let the sea roar, and its fullness!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let the field and all that is in it exult! Then all the trees of the woods shall sing for joy

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: before Yahweh; for he comes, for he comes to judge the earth. He will judge the world with righteousness, the peoples with his truth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh reigns! Let the earth rejoice! Let the multitude of islands be glad!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Clouds and darkness are around him. Righteousness and justice are the foundation of his throne.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A fire goes before him, and burns up his adversaries on every side.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His lightning lights up the world. The earth sees, and trembles.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The mountains melt like wax at the presence of Yahweh, at the presence of the Lord of the whole earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The heavens declare his righteousness. All the peoples have seen his glory.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let all them be shamed who serve engraved images, who boast in their idols. Worship him, all you gods!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Zion heard and was glad. The daughters of Judah rejoiced, because of your judgments, Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you, Yahweh, are most high above all the earth. You are exalted far above all gods.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You who love Yahweh, hate evil. He preserves the souls of his saints. He delivers them out of the hand of the wicked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Light is sown for the righteous, and gladness for the upright in heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Be glad in Yahweh, you righteous people! Give thanks to his holy Name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A Psalm. Sing to Yahweh a new song, for he has done marvelous things! His right hand, and his holy arm, have worked salvation for him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh has made known his salvation. He has openly shown his righteousness in the sight of the nations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has remembered his loving kindness and his faithfulness toward the house of Israel. All the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Make a joyful noise to Yahweh, all the earth! Burst out and sing for joy, yes, sing praises!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sing praises to Yahweh with the harp, with the harp and the voice of melody.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With trumpets and sound of the ram’s horn, make a joyful noise before the King, Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let the sea roar with its fullness; the world, and those who dwell therein.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let the rivers clap their hands. Let the mountains sing for joy together.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let them sing before Yahweh, for he comes to judge the earth. He will judge the world with righteousness, and the peoples with equity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh reigns! Let the peoples tremble. He sits enthroned among the cherubim. Let the earth be moved.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh is great in Zion. He is high above all the peoples.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let them praise your great and awesome name. He is Holy!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The King’s strength also loves justice. You do establish equity. You execute justice and righteousness in Jacob.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Exalt Yahweh our God. Worship at his footstool. He is Holy!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moses and Aaron were among his priests, Samuel among those who call on his name; they called on Yahweh, and he answered them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He spoke to them in the pillar of cloud. They kept his testimonies, the statute that he gave them.

translations.name: World English Bible
